value_chain_position: IP licensing layer — sells memory interface patents and PHY silicon (SerDes, HBM controllers) that every DRAM module and AI accelerator must license or buy.
relative_comps: RMBS is the near-monopoly licensor on DDR/HBM interface IP — a structural toll position most chip designers cannot route around, giving it 46% FCF margins that dwarf fabless peers like SMTC (15%) or MXL (2%). Revenue CAGR of ~16% trails hyper-growth names like CRDO but is more defensible given the royalty stream. Multiples are premium (mcap ~22x TTM revenue) but IP-licensor comps (Arm-class royalty dynamics) justify a structural discount to FCF-based peers. Main risk is customer concentration in major DRAM makers (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron).
